essence: Consumer durables are big-ticket, infrequent purchases: a fridge or TV bought every several years, so the game is brand trust, distribution, financing, and after-sales service - not repeat frequency.
Margins are modest and competition fierce; differentiation comes from brand, features/technology, and service network.
Demand is cyclical, linked to income, housing, and replacement cycles; financing (EMIs) unlocks affordability.
After-sales service and distribution reach are underrated moats - and a major purchase driver in markets like India.
Premiumisation and smart/connected features are the margin and growth levers.

definition.oneLiner: Companies that make and sell long-lasting household goods - appliances, electronics, furniture - bought infrequently and used for years.
definition.jobToBeDone: Equip homes with the long-lived products that make daily life easier - and stand behind them with service over their long life.

valueChain.profitPools: Profit pools sit with strong brands (trust for a big-ticket buy), with premium/connected segments, and increasingly with after-sales service and financing; commoditised, price-fought categories (entry TVs, basic appliances) are thin and pressured by imports/assemblers.
valueChain.powerShift: Value is shifting from pure product + offline distribution -> online/omnichannel + financing-led affordability -> premium, smart/connected products and services/after-sales, as components commoditise and brands seek differentiation.

unitEconomics.unitOfRevenue: Revenue per unit sold (high ticket, low frequency)
unitEconomics.revenueDrivers: Units x Price. Growth comes from volume (penetration, replacement, new homes), price/premiumisation, and financing that expands the affordable market.

costStructure.shape: Components/materials dominate COGS (often import-linked, FX-exposed), plus manufacturing, marketing, distribution, and a significant after-sales service cost; margins are modest and sensitive to input costs and competition.
costStructure.leverage: Scale in manufacturing and procurement plus premium mix improve margin; but high competition and commoditised components cap pricing, and demand cyclicality creates operating-leverage swings.

moats: Brand trust (critical for a big, infrequent purchase)
Distribution & service network (hard to build, drives purchase)
competitionAndMoats.concentration: Category-by-category: a few strong brands lead each (often global + strong local), with assemblers/importers and private labels pressuring the value end.

structuralForces.regulation: Moderate - energy-efficiency / BEE star ratings, safety standards, import duties & PLI (local-manufacturing push), e-waste rules.
structuralForces.cyclicality: Cyclical - tied to income, housing/real-estate, replacement cycles, and seasonality (e.g. AC in summer); discretionary big-ticket spend is deferrable in downturns.
structuralForces.substitution: Imports, assembler brands, and (for electronics) smartphones replacing standalone devices; rental/refurbished at the margin.
structuralForces.supplierBuyerPower: Component suppliers (chips/compressors/panels, often imported) hold power and FX risk; large retailers and online platforms pressure prices; consumers are price- and review-sensitive.

aiPenetration.maturity: Medium - AI in demand forecasting, service, and product features (smart/connected devices); manufacturing automation growing.
aiPenetration.valueChainImpact: AI's value is in supply chain/inventory (big-ticket, FX-exposed stock is costly), in after-sales (a real cost and loyalty driver), and in product differentiation via connected features; financing decisions also benefit from AI underwriting.
threatsDisruption: Commoditisation by assemblers + AI-optimised low-cost players

ranges: Financing-backed sales: a large & growing share in India
benchmarks.note: DIRECTIONAL - rules of thumb, not audited figures. Verify before quoting.

keyTerms.White goods: Large household appliances - fridges, washing machines, ACs.
keyTerms.Replacement cycle: Years between purchases of a durable - drives demand timing.
keyTerms.After-sales service: Repairs, warranty, spares - a cost centre and a key purchase driver.
keyTerms.Premium mix: Share of higher-end, higher-margin products in the sales mix.
keyTerms.BEE star rating: India's energy-efficiency label affecting product specs and demand.
keyTerms.PLI: Production-Linked Incentive - government scheme to boost local manufacturing.
keyTerms.Financing / EMI: Point-of-sale credit that makes big-ticket purchases affordable.
keyTerms.Assembler / importer: Low-cost brand that assembles/imports rather than manufactures deeply.

commonCaseTypes: Margin improvement amid input/FX cost pressure

questionsToAskFirst: What's the premium-mix share and trend?
How strong are distribution + after-sales networks vs peers?
Where in the demand/replacement cycle are we?
rookieTraps: Ignoring after-sales/service as a purchase driver and cost
Treating durables like FMCG (frequency vs big-ticket logic)
Missing the role of financing in demand

whatsUniqueHere: Consumer durables invert the FMCG logic: instead of frequent, habitual, low-stakes buys, these are rare, high-stakes, deliberated purchases - so the consumer leans hard on brand trust, reviews, financing, and the promise of service over a product's long life. That makes the after-sales and distribution network a genuine, under-appreciated moat, and financing (EMIs) the lever that quietly expands the entire addressable market in price-sensitive economies like India.

indiaLandscape.marketStructure: A large, under-penetrated, fast-growing market: global majors (Samsung, LG, Sony, Whirlpool, Haier) alongside strong Indian players (Voltas, Havells, Blue Star, Crompton, Bajaj) and contract manufacturers (Dixon); financing-led demand and a strong Make-in-India / PLI push toward local manufacturing.
demandDrivers: Low appliance penetration vs developed markets
indiaLandscape.note: Semi-perishable - players & policy specifics refresh periodically.

similarTo: Automotive - big-ticket, financed, brand + dealer/service network

currentState.note: PERISHABLE - refresh via research before quoting. Figures approximate.
currentState.marketStructure: India consumer durables: strong structural growth on low penetration; Make-in-India / PLI shifting manufacturing onshore (Dixon and others scaling as EMS); premiumisation and connected appliances rising; financing and quick-commerce/online expanding reach; intense competition keeps margins in check.
